judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 more clearly and forcefully calls out that migrations must be explicitly run/confirmed given the runbook's false claim, while Report 2 raises the issue but stops short of an explicit corrective recommendation; otherwise both reports are comparably thorough and accurate.

report_1.evidence: Line 4 — PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin": ... Omit entirely. ... Line 6 — SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...: Omit both SHADOW_SCORING_ENABLED and SHADOW_SCORING_SINK entirely. ... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ...: Omit entirely.

report_2.evidence: app.auto.tfvars:2 ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" ... Change to "false". ... app.auto.tfvars:4 PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS ... Remove it entirely (set to ""); no test account should have superadmin in prod.

report_1.evidence: `--mode staging` makes Vite load `frontend/.env.staging`, so **`VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` is baked into the shipped bundle** ... prod needs its own rebuild against a prod `VITE_API_BASE_URL`

report_2.evidence: The CI build (deploy.yml:14) runs npx vite build --mode staging — hardcoded to staging mode, with no separate prod build. So the shipped bundle always has https://staging-api.example.com baked in. ... The production frontend would therefore point at the staging API. To fix: add frontend/.env.production with the prod API URL and build the prod artifact with --mode production

report_1.evidence: No database migration step anywhere in the pipeline. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but no job invokes any migration ... For a first prod launch the schema won't exist yet — migrations must be run manually first; "pipeline succeeded" will not mean the schema is current.

report_2.evidence: Migrations: the runbook (prod-runbook.md:6) says DB migrations run automatically during deploy ... But migrations happen only inside the missing deploy.sh; there's no visible migration step or first-run seeding for a brand-new prod DB. Unverified whether prod's database is provisioned at all.

report_1.evidence: the condition is the **same copied string, `…environment:staging`** — it was not retargeted. ... It must be `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: But the prod role's condition still reads `...:environment:staging` (infra/prod/main.tf:3, uncorrected copy). ... Fix: change infra/prod/main.tf:3 to oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production".
